Penpot plugins API
    Preparing search index...
    • CommentThread

    Interface CommentThread

    Represents a list of comments one after the other. Usually these threads are conversations the users have in Penpot.

    interface CommentThread {
        seqNumber: number;
        board?: Board;
        owner?: User;
        position: Point;
        resolved: boolean;
        findComments(): Promise<Comment[]>;
        reply(content: string): Promise<Comment>;
        remove(): void;
    }
    • Defined in index.d.ts:598
    Index

    Properties

    seqNumber board? owner? position resolved

    Methods

    findComments reply remove

    Properties

    ReadonlyseqNumber

    seqNumber: number

    This is the number that is displayed on the workspace. Is an increasing sequence for each comment.

    • Defined in index.d.ts:603

    Optional Readonlyboard

    board?: Board

    If the thread is attached to a board this will have that board reference.

    • Defined in index.d.ts:609

    Optional Readonlyowner

    owner?: User

    Owner of the comment thread

    • Defined in index.d.ts:614

    position

    position: Point

    The position in absolute coordinates in the canvas.

    • Defined in index.d.ts:619

    resolved

    resolved: boolean

    Whether the thread has been marked as resolved or not.

    • Defined in index.d.ts:624

    Methods

    findComments

    • findComments(): Promise<Comment[]>

      List of comments ordered by creation date. Requires the comment:read or comment:write permission.

      Returns Promise<Comment[]>

      • Defined in index.d.ts:630

    reply

    • reply(content: string): Promise<Comment>

      Creates a new comment after the last one in the thread. The current user will be used as the creation user. Requires the comment:write permission.

      Parameters

      • content: string

      Returns Promise<Comment>

      • Defined in index.d.ts:637

    remove

    • remove(): void

      Removes the current comment thread. Only the user that created the thread can remove it. Requires the comment:write permission.

      Returns void

      • Defined in index.d.ts:644
